title: drosera cunninghamii
intro: Drosera cunninghamii, also known as the Western Sundew, is a carnivorous plant species native to Western Australia. It is known for its ability to capture and digest insects to supplement its nutrient intake due to its poor soil environment. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems like diarrhea. Drosera cunninghamii is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule in herbal supplements, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.
